title: Fix paste not found error after creation

requirements.text: Debug and fix the backend paste storage issue where paste ID 1771799269632900371 is not being found after creation. Verify that the createPaste function properly stores the paste data in the backend canister's state and that the getPaste function can retrieve it using the same ID format.

quotes: getting paste not found error after creating a paste link https://molecular-lavender-hsv-draft.caffeine.xyz/#/1771799269632900371

acceptanceCriteria: After creating a paste, navigating to its URL successfully displays the paste content
Backend getPaste function successfully retrieves pastes created by createPaste using the returned paste ID
No 'paste not found' errors occur for newly created pastes

requirements.text: Add backend logging to the createPaste and getPaste functions to track paste ID generation, storage operations, and retrieval attempts. Log the paste ID format, storage confirmation, and any retrieval failures with diagnostic information.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend logs show paste ID when a paste is created
Backend logs show storage confirmation after paste is saved
Backend logs show retrieval attempts with the requested paste ID and whether it was found

requirements.text: Verify that the paste ID type consistency between createPaste (which returns the ID) and getPaste (which accepts the ID as parameter) matches. Ensure both functions use the same ID representation (Nat vs Text vs custom type) throughout the storage and retrieval flow.
acceptanceCriteria: createPaste and getPaste use identical ID types
No type conversions or transformations occur between paste creation and retrieval that could cause ID mismatches
The paste storage map key type matches the ID type used in both functions

requirements.text: Add frontend error handling to display detailed backend error messages when a paste is not found, including the attempted paste ID and any backend diagnostic information returned in the error response.
acceptanceCriteria: PasteViewPage displays the attempted paste ID when showing 'not found' errors
Backend error messages are properly extracted and displayed to help diagnose the issue
Error state includes actionable information for debugging

constraints: Maintain backward compatibility with existing paste IDs
Do not modify the frontend paste ID validation logic which is working correctly
Preserve existing paste data structures and storage mechanisms
nonGoals: Changing the paste ID format or generation algorithm
Modifying the frontend routing or ID extraction logic
Adding new features beyond fixing the storage/retrieval bug
